---
title: "Phase 1: Marker Set Generation"
subtitle: "VCF to LD-pruned PLINK files and genotype matrix"
date: last-modified
format:
  html:
    embed-resources: true
---

## Overview

The first phase of the pipeline transforms a multi-sample VCF into LD-pruned
PLINK binary filesets and a numeric genotype matrix. These outputs feed into
phenotype simulation (Phase 2) and GWAS mapping (Phase 3).

The processing flow is:

```
VCF
 ├─ LOCAL_GET_CONTIG_INFO ──► contig_mapping.tsv, contigs.txt
 │                                    │
 ▼                                    │
 BCFTOOLS_EXTRACT_STRAINS             │
 ▼                                    │
 BCFTOOLS_RENAME_CHROMS ◄─────────────┘
 ▼
 PLINK_RECODE_MS_VCF ────────► TO_SIMS.* binaries + markers.txt (marker SNP set)
 PLINK_RECODE_CV_VCF ────────► CV_TO_SIMS.* binaries (causal variant pool)
 │                                    │
 ▼                                    ▼
 (to Phase 2/3)          BCFTOOLS_CREATE_GENOTYPE_MATRIX ──► genotype matrix TSV
                                       │
                          (DB path — see callout notes below)
                          ├─ DB_MIGRATION_WRITE_MARKER_SET ──► marker_sets/{id}_markers.parquet
                          │    (requires .bim + n_indep_tests from EIGEN)
                          └─ DB_MIGRATION_WRITE_GENOTYPE_MATRIX ──► genotypes/{id}_genotypes.parquet
```

Each process is described below with the exact commands run, key parameters,
and links to external tool documentation.


## LOCAL_GET_CONTIG_INFO (upstream helper)

**Source:** `modules/local/get_contig_info/main.nf`

Parses `##contig` header lines from the VCF to build a name-to-numeric mapping
(e.g. `I` &rarr; `1`, `II` &rarr; `2`, `MtDNA` &rarr; `7`). This mapping is
required because PLINK and GCTA expect numeric chromosome identifiers.

**Commands:**

```bash
# Extract ##contig headers and build tab-separated mapping (name → number)
zcat input.vcf.gz | head -n 200 | grep "##contig" | \
    awk 'BEGIN{COUNT=1}{split($1,A,"="); split(A[3],B,","); CHROM=B[1];
         printf "%s\t%i\n", CHROM, COUNT; COUNT=COUNT+1}' > contig_mapping.tsv

# Extract just the chromosome names
cut -f 1 contig_mapping.tsv > contigs.txt
```

**Outputs:**

| File                 | Description |
|----------------------|-------------|
| `contig_mapping.tsv` | Tab-separated file: chromosome name (col 1) and numeric ID (col 2) |
| `contigs.txt`        | One chromosome name per line (used by main.nf for mito filtering and chromosome list) |

**Used by:**

- `BCFTOOLS_RENAME_CHROMS` &mdash; consumes `contig_mapping.tsv` as the rename mapping file
- `main.nf` workflow logic &mdash; reads `contigs.txt` to identify the mitochondrial
  chromosome name and generate the list of autosomal chromosomes


## 1. BCFTOOLS_EXTRACT_STRAINS

**Source:** `modules/bcftools/extract_strains/main.nf`

Subsets the VCF to only the strains in the current strain set and removes any
sites with missing genotypes. This produces a clean, strain-specific VCF for
downstream processing.

**Commands:**

```bash
# Subset samples and filter missing sites, then index
bcftools view --threads ${task.cpus} -s ${strains} -O u ${vcf} | \
bcftools filter -i N_MISSING=0 -O z -o ${group}.vcf.gz
bcftools index --tbi ${group}.vcf.gz
```

| Command | Purpose | Docs |
|---------|---------|------|
| [`bcftools view -s`](https://samtools.github.io/bcftools/bcftools.html#view) | Subset to specified sample names | bcftools view |
| [`bcftools filter -i N_MISSING=0`](https://samtools.github.io/bcftools/bcftools.html#filter) | Keep only sites where no sample has a missing genotype | bcftools filter |
| [`bcftools index --tbi`](https://samtools.github.io/bcftools/bcftools.html#index) | Create a tabix (`.tbi`) index for the output VCF | bcftools index |

**Key flags:**

- `-O u` &mdash; pipe as uncompressed BCF (faster for piping between bcftools commands)
- `-O z` &mdash; write bgzf-compressed VCF output

**Inputs / Outputs:**

| Direction | File | Description |
|-----------|------|-------------|
| Input | `*.vcf.gz` + `.tbi` | Raw input VCF (original, before chromosome renaming) |
| Input | `strains` (val) | Comma-separated list of strain names |
| Output | `${group}.vcf.gz` + `.tbi` | Strain-subset, no-missing-site VCF with tabix index |


## 2. BCFTOOLS_RENAME_CHROMS

**Source:** `modules/bcftools/rename_chroms/main.nf`

Renames chromosome identifiers from text (I, II, III, ...) to numeric (1, 2,
3, ...) using the mapping file produced by `LOCAL_GET_CONTIG_INFO`. Numeric
chromosome names are required by PLINK and GCTA.

**Commands:**

```bash
# Stream VCF, rename chromosomes, compress and index
bcftools view --threads ${task.cpus} -O u ${vcf} | \
bcftools annotate --rename-chrs chr_mapping.tsv -O z -o ${id}_renamed.vcf.gz
bcftools index --tbi ${id}_renamed.vcf.gz
```

| Command | Purpose | Docs |
|---------|---------|------|
| [`bcftools view`](https://samtools.github.io/bcftools/bcftools.html#view) | Stream VCF as uncompressed BCF for piping | bcftools view |
| [`bcftools annotate --rename-chrs`](https://samtools.github.io/bcftools/bcftools.html#annotate) | Rename chromosomes using a tab-separated mapping file | bcftools annotate |
| [`bcftools index --tbi`](https://samtools.github.io/bcftools/bcftools.html#index) | Create tabix index | bcftools index |

**Mapping file format:** Tab-separated, one line per chromosome &mdash;
old name in column 1, new name in column 2:

```
I       1
II      2
III     3
IV      4
V       5
X       6
MtDNA   7
```

**Inputs / Outputs:**

| Direction | File | Description |
|-----------|------|-------------|
| Input | `*.vcf.gz` + `.tbi` | Source VCF (from input channel or `BCFTOOLS_EXTRACT_STRAINS`) |
| Input | `chr_mapping.tsv` | Chromosome name mapping from `LOCAL_GET_CONTIG_INFO` |
| Output | `*_renamed.vcf.gz` + `.tbi` | VCF with numeric chromosome names |


## 3. PLINK_RECODE_MS_VCF / PLINK_RECODE_CV_VCF

**Source:** `modules/plink/recode_vcf/main.nf` (aliased twice in `main.nf`)

The same module is imported under two alias names and called in parallel for
each strain group:

- **`PLINK_RECODE_MS_VCF`** — marker SNP set run. Uses per-group `ms_maf` and `ms_ld`
  thresholds from the strainfile. Outputs `TO_SIMS.*` files used for GRM construction
  and GWA mapping (Phase 3).
- **`PLINK_RECODE_CV_VCF`** — causal variant pool run. Uses `params.cv_maf` (defaults to
  the group's `ms_maf` if not set) and `params.cv_ld` thresholds. Outputs `CV_TO_SIMS.*`
  files used for causal variant selection in Phase 2.

Both runs perform LD pruning and produce PLINK binary filesets through two sequential
PLINK commands: first to identify LD-pruned variants, then to create the binary files
using only those variants.

### Command 1: LD pruning

```bash
plink --vcf recoded.vcf.gz \
    --snps-only \
    --biallelic-only \
    --maf ${maf} \
    --set-missing-var-ids @:# \
    --indep-pairwise 50 10 ${ld} \
    --geno \
    --not-chr ${mito_name} \
    --allow-extra-chr
```

This command computes pairwise LD and produces `plink.prune.in` (variant IDs to
retain) and `plink.prune.out` (variant IDs to remove).

| Flag | Purpose | Docs |
|------|---------|------|
| `--snps-only` | Exclude indels, retain only SNPs | [plink filter](https://www.cog-genomics.org/plink/1.9/filter#snps_only) |
| `--biallelic-only` | Exclude multiallelic sites | [plink filter](https://www.cog-genomics.org/plink/1.9/filter#biallelic) |
| `--maf ${maf}` | Remove variants below the minor allele frequency threshold | [plink filter](https://www.cog-genomics.org/plink/1.9/filter#maf) |
| `--set-missing-var-ids @:#` | Assign IDs to variants missing an rsID, using `CHROM:POS` format | [plink data](https://www.cog-genomics.org/plink/1.9/data#set_missing_var_ids) |
| `--indep-pairwise 50 10 ${ld}` | Sliding-window LD pruning: window=50 variants, step=10, r^2^ threshold from `ld` input (per-group `ms_ld` for the MS run; `cv_ld` for the CV run) | [plink LD](https://www.cog-genomics.org/plink/1.9/ld#indep) |
| `--geno` | Exclude variants with missing genotype rates above the default threshold (0.1) | [plink filter](https://www.cog-genomics.org/plink/1.9/filter#missing) |
| `--not-chr ${mito_name}` | Exclude the mitochondrial chromosome | [plink filter](https://www.cog-genomics.org/plink/1.9/filter#chr) |
| `--allow-extra-chr` | Permit non-standard chromosome codes | [plink filter](https://www.cog-genomics.org/plink/1.9/filter#chr) |

### Command 2: Binary fileset creation

```bash
plink --vcf recoded.vcf.gz \
    --make-bed \
    --snps-only \
    --biallelic-only \
    --maf ${maf} \
    --set-missing-var-ids @:# \
    --extract plink.prune.in \
    --geno \
    --recode \
    --out TO_SIMS \
    --allow-extra-chr
```

Re-applies the same filtering flags for consistency and additionally:

| Flag | Purpose | Docs |
|------|---------|------|
| `--make-bed` | Create PLINK binary fileset (`.bed`, `.bim`, `.fam`) | [plink data](https://www.cog-genomics.org/plink/1.9/data#make_bed) |
| `--extract plink.prune.in` | Retain only variants that passed LD pruning | [plink data](https://www.cog-genomics.org/plink/1.9/data#extract) |
| `--recode` | Also produce text-format `.ped`/`.map` files | [plink data](https://www.cog-genomics.org/plink/1.9/data#recode) |
| `--out TO_SIMS` | Set output file prefix | &mdash; |

### Post-processing: markers.txt

```bash
awk -F":" '$1=$1' OFS="\t" plink.prune.in | \
    sort -k1,1d -k2,2n > markers.txt
```

Converts the `CHROM:POS` variant IDs from `plink.prune.in` into a
tab-separated `CHROM  POS` file, sorted by chromosome then position. This
marker list is used by `BCFTOOLS_CREATE_GENOTYPE_MATRIX` to subset the VCF.

### Inputs / Outputs

| Direction | File | Description |
|-----------|------|-------------|
| Input | `recoded.vcf.gz` + `.tbi` | Chromosome-renamed, strain-subset VCF |
| Input | `mito_name` (val) | Numeric mitochondrial chromosome ID (looked up from `LOCAL_GET_CONTIG_INFO` mapping using the `--mito_name` parameter; e.g., `7` for MtDNA) |
| Input | `maf` (val) | Minor allele frequency threshold (`ms_maf` for the MS run; `cv_maf` or fallback `ms_maf` for the CV run) |
| Input | `ld` (val) | LD R² pruning threshold (`ms_ld` for the MS run; `cv_ld` for the CV run) |
| Output (MS run) | `TO_SIMS.bed/.bim/.fam` | PLINK binary fileset for GRM construction and GWA mapping (Phase 3) |
| Output (MS run) | `TO_SIMS.map/.ped` | PLINK text-format fileset |
| Output (MS run) | `TO_SIMS.nosex/.log` | PLINK auxiliary outputs |
| Output (CV run) | `CV_TO_SIMS.bed/.bim/.fam` | PLINK binary fileset for causal variant selection (Phase 2) |
| Output (CV run) | `CV_TO_SIMS.map/.ped` | PLINK text-format fileset |
| Output (CV run) | `CV_TO_SIMS.nosex/.log` | PLINK auxiliary outputs |
| Output (MS run) | `recoded.vcf.gz` + `.tbi` | Copy of the input VCF (passed through for downstream use) |
| Output (MS run) | `markers.txt` | Tab-separated list of retained markers (CHROM, POS) |

::: {.callout-note}
The `TO_SIMS.bim` file (MS run output) is consumed by `DB_MIGRATION_WRITE_MARKER_SET`,
which writes the LD-pruned marker list to
`{db}/markers/marker_sets/{marker_set_id}_markers.parquet`. `marker_set_id` is the
20-character SHA-256 hash (v=2) computed from `(population, maf, species, vcf_release_id,
ms_ld)` by `generate_marker_set_id()` in `R/database.R`. `DB_MIGRATION_WRITE_MARKER_SET`
also requires `n_indep_tests` (number of independent tests from EIGEN analysis) before it
can run; it stores this alongside the marker list in `marker_set_metadata.parquet`.
`n_indep_tests` is computed by `R_FIND_GENOTYPE_MATRIX_EIGEN` using full eigendecomposition
of the genotype correlation matrix per chromosome (Li & Ji 2005): for each eigenvalue λ,
contribute 1 if λ > 1, else the fractional part of λ. Chromosome counts are summed by
`LOCAL_COMPILE_EIGENS`.

The `CV_TO_SIMS.*` binary (CV run output) is not written to the database. It is passed to
Phase 2 (`PYTHON_SIMULATE_EFFECTS_GLOBAL`) for causal variant selection, then forwarded to
`GCTA_SIMULATE_PHENOTYPES` as the `--bfile` source. Only the MS binary drives GWA mapping.
:::


## 4. BCFTOOLS_CREATE_GENOTYPE_MATRIX

**Source:** `modules/bcftools/create_genotype_matrix/main.nf`

Creates a numeric genotype matrix (markers &times; strains) from the VCF,
restricted to the LD-pruned marker set. Genotypes are encoded numerically for
use in eigenvalue decomposition and downstream R analysis.

**Commands:**

```bash
# List and sort sample names
bcftools query -l ${vcf} | sort > sorted_samples.txt

# Extract genotypes for pruned markers, recode to numeric
bcftools view -v snps -S sorted_samples.txt -R ${markers} ${vcf} | \
    bcftools query --print-header -f '%CHROM\t%POS\t%REF\t%ALT[\t%GT]\n' | \
    sed 's/[[# 0-9]*]//g' | \   # clean header annotations
    sed 's/:GT//g' | \           # remove :GT suffix from sample names
    sed 's/0|0/-1/g' | \         # REF/REF homozygous → -1
    sed 's/1|1/1/g' | \          # ALT/ALT homozygous →  1
    sed 's/0|1/NA/g' | \         # heterozygous → NA
    sed 's/1|0/NA/g' | \         # heterozygous → NA
    sed 's/.|./NA/g' | \         # missing → NA
    sed 's/0\/0/-1/g' | \        # (unphased) REF/REF → -1
    sed 's/1\/1/1/g' | \         # (unphased) ALT/ALT →  1
    sed 's/0\/1/NA/g' | \        # (unphased) het → NA
    sed 's/1\/0/NA/g' | \        # (unphased) het → NA
    sed 's/.\/./NA/g' \          # (unphased) missing → NA
    > ${group}_${maf}_Genotype_Matrix.tsv
```

| Command | Purpose | Docs |
|---------|---------|------|
| [`bcftools query -l`](https://samtools.github.io/bcftools/bcftools.html#query) | List sample names from the VCF header | bcftools query |
| [`bcftools view -v snps -S ... -R ...`](https://samtools.github.io/bcftools/bcftools.html#view) | Subset to SNPs, reorder samples, restrict to marker regions | bcftools view |
| [`bcftools query -f`](https://samtools.github.io/bcftools/bcftools.html#query) | Extract fields in a custom tab-separated format | bcftools query |

### Genotype encoding

| Genotype | Encoding | Meaning |
|----------|----------|---------|
| `0\|0` or `0/0` | `-1` | Homozygous reference |
| `1\|1` or `1/1` | `1` | Homozygous alternate |
| `0\|1`, `1\|0`, `0/1`, `1/0` | `NA` | Heterozygous |
| `.\|.`, `./.` | `NA` | Missing |

**Design note:** The encoding assumes inbred diploid organisms. *C. elegans*
strains are expected to be homozygous, so heterozygous calls are treated as
missing data (`NA`) rather than given an intermediate value.

### Inputs / Outputs

| Direction | File | Description |
|-----------|------|-------------|
| Input | `recoded.vcf.gz` + `.tbi` | Chromosome-renamed, strain-subset VCF |
| Input | `markers.txt` | LD-pruned marker positions from `PLINK_RECODE_MS_VCF` |
| Output | `${group}_${maf}_Genotype_Matrix.tsv` | Tab-separated genotype matrix: rows are markers (CHROM, POS, REF, ALT), columns are strains |

::: {.callout-note}
This TSV is consumed by two downstream paths: the legacy path (`R_GET_GCTA_INTERVALS`,
`R_ASSESS_SIMS`) uses it for variance explained estimation; the DB path
(`DB_MIGRATION_WRITE_GENOTYPE_MATRIX`) pivots it to long format and stores it in
`{db}/markers/genotypes/{marker_set_id}_genotypes.parquet` for future offline
analysis. `marker_set_id` is the 20-character SHA-256 hash of the population and
MAF threshold, computed by `generate_marker_set_id()` in `R/database.R`.
:::
